because.text: Successor of the v0.6.5 set 8cfc2c63. Conformance-hardening PATCH: GOV-anchors.md 1.0.1 -> 1.0.2, §3 steps 2-3 now spell out JCS byte-canonicality of every blob/record on the wire and closure id-soundness (a record is a graph node only if filename WarrantID == sha256(canon(body)), Warrant §3), plus rejection of trailing content after a JSON value — invariants §2 and Warrant §3 already mandated but the reference implementations under-enforced (Python settlement_closure admitted id-unsound bridges; Go parseJSONBlob admitted trailing JSON; neither byte-checked canonicality). Surfaced by an external Codex adversarial audit. All 17 prior governance vectors byte-identical; three adversarial vectors added (17 -> 20); Go gov-replay 20/20, differential 27/27, both implementations agree. Independently gated: DeepSeek v4 Pro + Gemini 3.1 Pro both APPROVE-PATCH (a conformance fix to already-normative text, not a @v2 mechanism change); codex CLI was unavailable (blocked by its vendor content filter on the repo security-testing vocabulary). Only GOV-anchors.md and governance_vectors.json anchors change. Filed by the maintainer; second roster signature pending — authorizes nothing until 2-of-3.

subject.note: ADOPT v0.6.6 anchor-set (GOV 1.0.2: JCS byte-canonicality + closure id-soundness + trailing-content refusal made explicit in §3; 3 adversarial vectors; independently gated conformance PATCH)
